steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Form, Structure, and Sense question testing the introductory modifier rule. The passage ends with "Fictional yet realistic," followed by a blank. An introductory adjective phrase must be immediately followed by the noun it describes as the grammatical subject of the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — these personas include specific details (demographics, browsing habits, etc.) that UX designers draw from actual users.: subject is "these personas." Personas are the fictional yet realistic characters described in the passage. The modifier correctly attaches to the subject it actually describes. ✓
  • Choice B — specific details that UX designers draw from actual users (demographics, browsing habits, etc.) are included in these personas.: subject is "specific details." Specific details (demographics, browsing habits) are not fictional yet realistic characters — personas are. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — UX designers include specific details (demographics, browsing habits, etc.) drawn from actual users in these personas.: subject is "UX designers." UX designers are real people, not fictional yet realistic characters. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — actual users are the source of the specific details (demographics, browsing habits, etc.) that UX designers include in these personas.: subject is "actual users." Actual users are real, not fictional. Dangling modifier. ✗
A is correct.
